name: Landing Page Optimisation Checklist
intro: Audit your landing page against proven conversion rate optimisation (CRO) best practices to maximise lead generation and sales from paid and organic traffic.

seo.title: Landing Page Checklist — CRO Best Practices | Varada Nexus
seo.description: Free landing page checklist. Audit your landing page against conversion rate optimisation best practices for better lead generation.

items.text: Clear headline that states the value proposition
Sub-headline supports headline with specific benefit
Hero image or video relevant to offer
Primary CTA button visible without scrolling
No navigation menu (reduces distraction)
Customer testimonials or case studies included
Social proof numbers (clients, reviews, years) shown
Trust badges (certifications, awards, media mentions) visible
Privacy assurance near form/CTA
Form has minimal required fields (≤5)
CTA copy is action-oriented and benefit-led
Form above the fold or immediately after value prop
Confirmation / thank you page set up with next step
Benefits (not just features) clearly listed
Objection handling / FAQ section included
Urgency or scarcity element present (if appropriate)
Contact details / live chat available
Page loads in under 3 seconds on mobile
Page is mobile-responsive
Conversion tracking pixel / goal set up
A/B test running or planned

assumptions: Critical items are those that most directly impact conversion rate based on CRO research.
disclaimer: This tool provides an indicative estimate for general planning only and is not professional, legal, financial, medical or engineering advice. Reference values are editable defaults that vary by location, vendor, regulation and date. Verify critical figures with a qualified professional. Varada Nexus accepts no liability for decisions made using this tool.

faq.q: What is the most important element of a landing page?
faq.a: The headline is the single most impactful element — it determines whether a visitor stays. It must immediately communicate the key benefit for the specific audience.

faq.q: How long should a landing page be?
faq.a: Short pages (300–500 words, single CTA) work for warm traffic; longer pages (1,000–2,000 words) with multiple CTAs work better for cold traffic needing more convincing.

faq.q: What conversion rate should I expect from a landing page?
faq.a: Industry average is 2–5% for general landing pages; optimised pages for warm audiences can achieve 10–20% or higher. Test, measure and iterate.
